name: Hip Abduction Machine
resistance_profile: Isolates the gluteus medius/minimus via hip abduction.
execution_steps: Sit in the machine with your back against the pad and set the resistance.
Place the outside of your knees against the pads and sit tall.
Push your knees outward against the pads as far as comfortable.
Pause and squeeze the outer glutes at the end of the movement.
Return your knees slowly under control without letting the weight slam.
good_when: You want to directly target the gluteus medius/minimus (upper and outer glutes).
You want a low-fatigue, easy-to-learn accessory to round out glute training.
You are building hip stability with controlled abduction.
bad_when: You want maximal glute size and strength (choose hip thrusts or squats).
You are short on time and need one compound lift rather than an isolation accessory.
